items.hook: Hindumanes is a genus of jumping spiders found in India.
items.body: It was first described by Dmitri Logunov in 2004. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Grave of the Vampire is a 1972 American vampire film directed by John Hayes, and starring William Smith, Michael Pataki, and Lyn Peters.
items.body: Its plot follows a vampire who rapes a living woman, resulting in the birth of a child who feeds only on blood. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Trump drops BBC's commercial arm from Panorama lawsuit, but main case remains
items.body: The US president removes BBC Studios from his defamation lawsuit over a controversial documentary.

items.hook: "The Yellow Rose of Texas" is a traditional American song dating back to at least the 1850s.
items.body: Members of the Western Writers of America chose it as one of the Top 100 Western songs of all time. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Ava DuVernay to make Netflix documentary 14th on birthright citizenship
items.body:

items.hook: The 19th century in science saw the birth of science as a profession; the term scientist was coined in 1833 by William Whewell, which soon replaced the older term of (natural) philosopher.

items.hook: Oscar-winning actress Brenda Fricker dies at 81
items.body: She won for her role in 1989 film My Left Foot and had well-loved parts in Home Alone 2 and TV's Casualty.

items.hook: The Miami Hurricanes football team represents the University of Miami in college football.
items.body: The program began in 1926. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
